summary:
Lenacapavir is an FDA-approved long-acting integrase strand transfer inhibitor specifically indicated for treating HIV in adults. Clinical evidence confirms its efficacy and favorable safety profile when compared to established integrase inhibitors such as dolutegravir and raltegravir. 

The summary should end with one sentence about whether or not lenacapavir can be used for HIV based solely on this reasoning.

Lenacapavir can be used for HIV treatment.  

(Do NOT include any additional commentary.)

Lenacapavir is an FDA-approved long-acting integrase strand transfer inhibitor specifically indicated for treating HIV in adults. Its inclusion among well-established integrase inhibitors and confirmed safety profile validate its appropriate use in current anti-HIV regimens.  
Lenacapavir can be used for HIV.

Lenacapavir can be used for HIV.
